                                                                   Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100385



                                                                  Contents lists available at ScienceDirect


                                                           Egyptian Informatics Journal
                                                         journal homepage: www.sciencedirect.com




Toward secret data location via fuzzy logic and convolutional neural
network
Ntivuguruzwa Jean De La Croix a,b, Tohari Ahmad a,⇑
a
    Department of Informatics, Institut Teknologi Sepuluh Nopember (ITS), Kampus ITS Keputih Sukolilo, Surabaya 60111, Indonesia
b
    African Center of Excellence in the Internet of Things, College of Science and Technology, University of Rwanda, Kigali 3900, Rwanda




a r t i c l e           i n f o                            a b s t r a c t

Article history:                                           Locating hidden data in digital images, otherwise called steganalysis, is a process of identifying the exis-
Received 16 March 2023                                     tence of secret messages within digital images. Steganalysis is used to manage digital data transmission
Revised 9 May 2023                                         by detecting the possible hidden information that can be used to violate the network policy; hence, it
Accepted 22 May 2023
                                                           helps the development of policies and regulations aimed at strong protection from cyber threats to indi-
Available online 30 May 2023
                                                           viduals’ and organizations’ data. The research works in the field of information security commonly focus
                                                           on developing the locating approaches for non-adaptive steganography, which present a problem of less
Keywords:
                                                           investigation of the complex challenge of locating the payload embedded with an adaptive stegano-
Fuzzy logic
Spatial domain
                                                           graphic algorithm. In this article, we propose a method to locate hidden data in a digital image in three
CNN                                                        stages: a) Identification of the modification maps between the carrier and final images. b) Using the mod-
Network infrastructure                                     ification maps as input to Mamdani fuzzy inference with four input membership functions: covariance
Steganalysis                                               map matrix, compass mean matrix, distance vector matrix, and pixel intensity matrix, and one output
Information security                                       membership function, notably the fuzzy correlation maps. c) Feeding the fuzzy correlation maps to a con-
                                                           volutional neural network to identify the pixels with confidential data from the innocent pixels. By exper-
                                                           imenting with our method against four steganographic algorithms, namely, HILL, HUGO-BD, WOW, and
                                                           S-UNIWARD, the recall rates for the four algorithms initially increase in a similar range and improve with
                                                           increasing payload capacity, which justifies the outperformance of the proposed strategy over the exist-
                                                           ing methods.
                                                            Ó 2023 THE AUTHORS. Published by Elsevier BV on behalf of Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intel-
                                                              ligence, Cairo University. This is an open access article under the CC BY-NC-ND license (http://creative-
                                                                                                                                  commons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/).




1. Introduction                                                                                developed to detect the presence of secret information in a digital
                                                                                               image or even extract confidential data. This counter-art to
    Steganography is a subdomain of the information security                                   steganography has been referred to as steganalysis.
domain which mainly works on ensuring the covert transmission                                     For any steganographic scheme, the main purpose is to maxi-
of secret messages using multimedia content as carriers. Generally,                            mize the imperceptibility of additional data in the content, making
a steganographic technique adopts data-hiding methods to conceal                               a cover media. In digital images, the data are hidden, avoiding any
the confidential data in the spatial content of an original digital                            visual or statistical distortion of the stego by ensuring its closest
medium, such as an image referred to as a cover to result in a                                 similarity to the cover image. In the past decade, various modern
transformed image, also referred to as a stego [2,7,8,30]. The recent                          steganographic schemes for both spatial and JPEG domains have
two decades marked a significant advancement of steganography                                  been presented; among them, the mostly known spatial adaptive
in digital images where various schemes have been proposed for                                 steganographic schemes we can say HIgh-pass Low-pass Low-
content-adaptive paradigms [3,11,19]. The adaptive stegano-                                    pass (HILL) (B [24]. Highly Undetectable steGO Gibbs construction
graphic models mainly consider confidential data as a fidelity con-                            with Bounding Distortion (HUGO-BD) [13], Wavelet Obtained
straint for the source coding practice rather than considering the                             Weights (WOW) [17], the Spatial version of the UNIversal WAvelet
carrier media. A reverse scheme to steganography has also been                                 Relative Distortion (S-UNIWARD) [18], and among the JPEG
                                                                                               domain we can say Uniform Embedding Distortion (UED) (Linjie
                                                                                               [26], Uniform Embedding Revisited Distortion (UERD) [16], and
    ⇑ Corresponding author.
                                                                                               JPEG Universal WAvelet Relative Distortion (J-UNIWARD) [16].
    E-mail addresses: 7025221024@mhs.its.ac.id (N.J. De La Croix), tohari@if.its.ac.id
(T. Ahmad).

    Based on the high imperceptibility of a stego image, which is a               The main tasks performed in our work to accomplish our aims
challenge to detect its nature, it is impossible to distinguish with a        and reach the targeted theoretical contributions are listed as
human eye between the cover and stego image; hence the main                   follows:
task of a steganalyst is to identify a cover from a stego image. Ste-
ganalysis is mainly classified into three categories based on the               (1) Based on the intrinsic features of modern adaptive steganog-
output of the process. A steganalysis process that outputs image                    raphy, we propose a steganalysis scheme that departs from
classification (as cover or stego) is known as detective steganalysis               the modification map between the cover and the stego as
or binary classification [21]; a steganalysis scheme that targets to                initial input to the fuzzy inference system that then provides
provide the locations of the hidden data is known as locative ste-                  the fuzzy correlation maps that are used for classification in
ganalysis [20]; a steganalysis scheme that aims to predict the pay-                 a CNN, unlike the existing approaches, which input a whole
load size is also known as quantitative steganalysis [29]; and a                    image.
steganalysis process that aims to extract the secret bits of the con-           (2) To improve the location accuracy by narrowing down errors,
fidential message is called forensic steganalysis [41]. In the existing             we apply fuzzy logic on the modification maps to generate
works on steganalysis, several researchers focused on the binary                    the fingerprint matrix (fuzzy correlation maps) to increase
classification of inquiry images into cover or stego [6,33,47], and                 the precision of the relationship between a pixel and its
some others focused on locative steganalysis but mainly working                     neighbors.
with LSB replacement and LSB matching [36,42] due to the possi-                 (3) We train our CNN with fuzzy correlation maps to address
bility of discovering the impurities with these types of steganogra-                the scarcity of the training images and the high cost of sev-
phy. Based on the use of Rich Models (RM), and Ensemble                             eral operations needed to preprocess the training images to
Classifiers (EC), most of the state-of-the-art [39,44,45] showed a                  yield reliable payload location accuracy.
significant performance in classifying images into cover and stego              (4) We conduct numerical experiments considering two scenar-
and relying on the deep learning paradigm, the steganalysis of dig-                 ios to locate the steganographic payload, notably known and
ital images knew a gradual widespread [6,35,47].                                    unknown steganographic payload scenarios. Our method is
    Many prior steganalysis algorithms have given much attention                    tested with HILL, HUGO-BD, WOW, and S-UNIWARD.
to detective steganalysis, and tasks like steganographic payload
location and payload size prediction have yet to be explored con-                The next parts of this paper are as follows: In Section 2, we dis-
siderably. Hence in this study, we mainly focus on the payload                cuss the existing literature focusing on the features of the current
location process based on the general idea of steganalysis. Further-          methods and their challenges. Section 3 describes our approach
more, based on the cover image prediction (or simply computing                and the proposed model, followed by the presentation and discus-
for differences between the confirmed stego and the cover images),            sion of the experimental results in Section 4. In Section 5, we con-
several works have been proposed targeting a specific type of                 clude our works and suggest the future takes in line with our
steganography, such as Least Significant Bit Replacement (LSBR)               model.
or the Least Significant Bit Matching (LSBM) [22,23,32] to locate
the steganographic payloads which become invalid when these
targeting algorithms are applied for universal payload location.              2. Related works
Moreover, keeping the same generality, the problem of secret data
location, which is also considered as a binary classification depart-             Dynamism and innovative discoveries in steganographic algo-
ing from a threshold value to classify image’s pixels into altered            rithms made it imperative to the counter art to steganography
and innocent pixels, most prior locative steganalysis schemes show            for forensic purposes to adapt the same speed to avail new ste-
invalidity when handling modern versions of adaptive steganogra-              ganalysis approaches. In recent years, the steganalysis of digital
phy which is also a critical limitation with them [20,27,31].                 images improved significantly due to the introduction of CNNs in
    Our study proposes a steganalysis model that uses fuzzy logic             solving the classification problem of images into cover (when they
and CNN to detect confidential data’s location in stego images uni-           don’t hold any confidential data) or stego (When they are used as
versally. Our main goals are to provide: i) a universal steganalysis          carriers for sensitive information) classes. Several steganalysis
approach to locate the payload embedded using an adaptive                     techniques, such as detective steganalysis to detect the presence
steganographic algorithm departing from only one inquiry image,               of confidential data in an image [33,38,47], quantitative steganaly-
ii) an improved precision while exploring the relationship between            sis [5,41,46] to estimate the payload size have been proposed.
the inquiry image’s pixels, and iii) improved accuracy in locating            These techniques are mainly used to identify any alteration in
the altered pixels (pixels holding steganographic payloads) using             the pixels of an image when adding some confidential bits. In fact,
the binary classification paradigm of deep learning. To achieve               they achieved a promising performance but have not been able to
our goals, we depart from the common idea of modern adaptive                  identify the location of the altered pixel, which remains a vital
steganography to conceal the data in the image’s texture region.              practice for digital image forensics. Steganographic payload loca-
Our method combines fuzzy logic to generate fuzzy correlation                 tion has become the most important form of steganalysis, which
maps, which make a fingerprint matrix of an image and a CNN to                enables the classification of image pixels based on whether they
predict areas with confidential data. Specifically, i) we initially           have been changed by steganography. As per other forms of ste-
use Syndrome-trellis codes (STCs) [14] to embed the data with a               ganalysis, this locative steganalysis also adopts the paradigm of
modern adaptive steganography algorithm in a cover image to                   binary classification among the image’s pixels which is rooted in
obtain a stego image and calculate the modification map between               the traditional machine learning pattern recognition algorithms
the cover and the stego, ii) we use Mamdani fuzzy inference sys-              and adopted in CNN-based steganalysis tasks as of Fig. 1.
tem to compute for the fuzzy correlation maps of the modification                 Liu et al. (2015) introduced a steganalysis scheme to locate con-
maps which enhances the precision in exploring the relationship               cealed information using steganography in the spatial domain. The
between the inquiry image’s pixels, and iii) we consider the fuzzy            method involves the retrieval of most cover pixels through recom-
correlation maps for classification using CNN which yields an accu-           pression, then analyzing the differences in pixel distribution
rate binary classification of the pixels of the inquiry image (refer-         between the cover and stego images. This scheme finally employs
ring to the paradigm by Yalcinkaya and Erbas [40].                            the hypothesis testing theory to prove the correctness of locating

the payload and uses this to generate a random bitstream. The per-             algorithm.
formance shown by the results identified an outperformance of the
existing methods.                                                              3.1. Significance of our algorithm
    Wang et al. (2020) presented a steganalysis scheme to locate
hidden data by constructing 64 co-frequency sub-images and fil-                    Our study introduces a novel locative steganalysis technique
tering the obtained sub-images. The proposed approach, which                   that employs a hybrid approach of fuzzy logic and CNN operations
concentrates on the JPEG domain, works on blocks of 8  8 in an                to identify the confidential data within an image’s pixels. By using
image by combining the same position coefficients of each block                fuzzy reasoning in the preprocessing phase of our strategy, we aim
to generate 64 co-frequency mini-images and then apply the Mar-                to improve the detection process’s accuracy and efficiency, enhanc-
kov model by using the maximum a posterior probability to deter-               ing our strategy’s overall effectiveness in the locative steganalysis
mine the estimated cover co-frequency mini-images. The                         task. Fuzzy logic is a scheme that mathematically allows for
computation of the dissimilarities between the probable cover                  decision-making in ambiguous and uncertain situations [1,28,15].
and the calculated residual of the discrete cosine transform coeffi-           The fuzzy correlation map calculation has been proved as a tech-
cient in the same cartesian position in multiple mini-images of the            nique in image processing to reduce noise effects and other image
stego type is mainly used to locate the altered parts of an inquiry            distortions [40]. The computation of fuzzy correlation maps is
image. Moreover, a new approach to locate the pixels with confi-               based on various components such as the covariance map matrix,
dential data hidden with JPEG steganography has been introduced                compass mean matrix, distance vector matrix, and pixel intensity
to mainly work on estimating the cover image by assigning various              matrix. We detail the description of the correlation maps calcula-

tion in Subsection 3. 3. It is worth noting that our strategy prefers        sive data embedments, checking whether a re-embedding selects
to use the modification maps between the two versions of the                 the same pixels again. We depart from a property that the cost
stego images based on the fact that modification maps present cap-           matrix does not change between the first and second stego images.
ital importance in recognizing the patterns and changes that are             The following stages define our stages to obtain the modified maps.
indicative of a steganography effect on the image’s content alter-
ation [31]. A step-by-step process to generate the modification                (1) We conceal the random secret data in the cover C to obtain
maps is further detailed in Subsection 3. 2. Referring to the existing             the first stego ST1.
literature, as described in the previous Section, it has been identi-          (2) We re-embed the same secret data in ST1 to obtain the sec-
fied that the utilization of CNNs in the steganalysis operations has               ond stego ST2.
become efficient and popular based on their capability to extract              (3) Using Syndrome-Trellis Codes (STCs), we calculate the cost
and learn the relevant features of images considered for steganal-                 matrix referring to [14], which guides us in selecting the
ysis. CNNs play a significant role in enabling the discovery of pat-               embeddable pixels to obtain ST1 and ST2.
terns and alterations in pixel intensities that show any                       (4) We define the modification maps Modmap for a pixel at ði; jÞ
steganographic change. To improve the ability of the CNN to per-                   referring to (1) as follows:
form the steganalysis operations, we chose to use it in combination

unique features of an image. A flow diagram of the proposed strat-

    Referring to the wide recognition of the neural networks in the          as of Fig. 3 with Modmap1 obtained from a comparative analysis
final features generating task, cover images are trained in two main         between the cover and ST1 and Modmap2 obtained from ST1 and
types of layers: the convolutional and the downsampling layers               ST2. It is also important to identify that two data concealments
combined with other crucial elements of a neural network. The                with an adaptive steganography algorithm choose pixels almost
convolutional operation aims to model the correlation between a              in the same region, known as a texture region. However, in a chal-
pixel and its neighboring. For example, if we set a kernel to size           lenging case where an algorithm does not always modify the same
3  3, the steganalysis features will be generated with 8 pixels sur-        pixels while there are neighboring pixels during two embedding
rounding it. Referring to the kernel size, which is usually constant         operations, we proceed with fuzzy logic on the secondly obtained
in most payload location CNN, two challenges can happen. The first           feature map to generate the fuzzy correlation maps.
is that with a large convolutional kernel size, information may be
redundant because of connecting many pixels, including some                  3.3. Fuzzy correlation maps generation
irrelevant pixels. The second is that a small kernel may also lose
some vital information.                                                         From Modmap2 we calculate the fuzzy correlation map with four
                                                                             basic input variables: the covariance map, the compass mean, the
3.2. Modification maps generation                                            distance vector matrix, and the pixel intensity matrix. The obtained
                                                                             fuzzy correlation map is set to a matrix of a dimension 256  256.
   The properties of adaptive steganography algorithms proved
the unchangeability of the pixels in a texture region, which means              A. Covariance map
that the same pixel is not changed two times, hence the preserva-
tion of the distribution of the cover content. We depart from those             The covariance map reflects how variables in pairs alter with
properties to identify the most probable pixels using two succes-            respect to each other. Covariance helps in data dependency model-

ing irrespective of their physical meaning. In an image, the covari-                                                2                      2
                                                                                       DV ði; jÞ ¼    ði  i0 Þ þ ðj  j0 Þ                                                              ð4Þ
ance is used to show the dependencies among the image’s statisti-
cal data, such as pixel position and intensity, and how they relate
to their derivatives. Based on their capital importance, the covari-
ance maps are primarily used to integrate the gathered data into a                        D. Pixel intensity matrix
deep learning framework [43]. Referring to [5], the covariance
cov ðx; yÞ for two variables x and y, whose respective means lx                           Departing from the modification map presented in a grayscale
and ly is got as of (2) using  a complex conjugate function.                          format because its pixels are set to values from 0 to 255 by (1).

    B. Compass-mean operation                                                          input. Fig. 4 shows the designed Mamdani fuzzy inference sys-
                                                                                       tem. It is worth noting that the fuzzy output matrix is a grayscale

make the operator sign or direct the pixels in its surroundings. It                    lated. It is also important to note that Fig. 5 shows a sample of
computes the correlation between the neighboring pixels and the                        some of the eighty-one fuzzy rules set based on fuzzy variable
pixel in the center to generate the mean intensities with fuzzy                        sets as of Fig. 6, which illustrates the input membership func-
logic. The compass mean calculation does not involve the center                        tions, and Fig. 7, which shows the resulting output membership
pixel to differentiate the compass mean from the average mean fil-                     function.
ters. The compass-mean C mean ði; jÞ for a pixel Pði:jÞ, with 1  i  227
and 1  j  227; i–j, at the cartesian position ði; jÞ is given by (3).                3.4. Description of the proposed CNN
                 1              X
C mean ði; jÞ ¼                        Pði; jÞ                                             The proposed CNN has four main layers: regular convolu-
                mn                                                          ð3Þ
                        1  m  1                                                     tional layers, average pooling layers, fully connected, and nor-
                        1  n  1                                                     malization layers. The overall architecture of the proposed CNN
                                                                                       is described in Table 1. In relation to the requirements and size
                                                                                       of the input, in the first convolutional layer, we use filters of
                                                                                       the size 11  11. The size of the input is 224  224  3, but we
    C. Distance vector matrix                                                          set it to 256  256  3 by padding with zeros. The convolutional
                                                                                       operation is independently applied to the filter and the input
   The distance vector matrix is a matrix of scalar values DVði; jÞ                    image, creating a two-dimensional feature map for each opera-
expressing the distance between a central pixel P0 ði0 ; j0 Þ and any                  tion. The objective of convolving the filter with the image is to
pixel at the position ði; jÞ computed by (4).                                          enable the filter to identify unique features in the image. To
                                                                                   5
N.J. De La Croix and T. Ahmad                                                                                      Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100385




                                                 Fig. 4. The designed Mamdani fuzzy inference system.




                                         Fig. 5. Sample rules among eight-one designed rules in the rule viewer.



increase the nonlinearity, we use a rectified Linear Unit Layer                3.5. Our approach to locating the suspicious pixels.
(ReLU), which is expected to enhance the network’s nonlinearity.
ReLU-based network training proves to be faster than tanh or                      Our algorithm is designed to locate the pixels that likely hold
sigmoid activation functions.                                                  the secret data added by adaptive steganography in the spatial
    The pooling layer shrinks the input image’s size through down-             domain. We have drawn inspiration from prior algorithms, and
sampling for the pooling operations. It divides the image into non-            our algorithm performs best when the stego is confirmed. Our
overlapping rectangles for preprocessing. We use two different                 algorithm is designed for two scenarios.
pooling operations: average pooling and maximum pooling. Aver-
age pooling plays a crucial role in CNNs by shrinking the size of the          1. In the first scenario, we assume that the steganographic payload
feature maps, thus lowering the required parameters and compu-                    is known, and we use the acquired payload to locate the modi-
tations. This speeds up training and enhances the network’s overall               fied pixels.
performance.                                                                   2. In the second scenario, we assume that the steganographic pay-
    Additionally, average pooling strengthens the feature maps,                   load is unknown, and we use quantitative strategies to estimate
curbs overfitting through its spatial invariance, and reduces sensi-              the payload and locate the modified pixels.
tivity to minor shifts in the input image. Switching to average pool-
ing from max pooling also increases the network’s invariance to                    The following steps can generally describe our algorithm:
slight variations in image intensity. Maximum pooling selects the                  1. Step 1: Generate a random bit stream.
maximum value of each rectangle to represent the lesion region.                    2. Step 2: Calculate the cost matrix of the cover image using the
By using maximum pooling, it is possible to achieve a dimensional              bank of designed filters based on an adaptive steganographic
reduction in the processed data.                                               algorithm.
    The softmax function and the fully connected layer in our                      3. Step 3: Embed a message using STCs to obtain the first stego
CNN collaborate to generate the final prediction. The fully con-               ST1 based on minimizing the distortion function using a cost
nected layer takes the output from the preceding layer, maxi-                  matrix.
mum pooling layer feature maps, and multiplies them by a set                       4. Step 4: Re-embed a message using STCs to obtain the second
of weights. This result goes through a ReLU to generate an inter-              stego ST2 based on the principle of minimizing the distortion func-
mediate output. The softmax function performs the final step in                tion using the cost matrix from step 2.
processing the fully connected layer’s output. This mathematical                   5. Step 5: Obtain the modification map between ST1 and ST2 as
operation transforms the intermediate result into a probability                illustrated in Fig. 3.
distribution across the output classes, namely innocent and                        6. Step 6: Calculate the correlation of neighboring pixels with
changed pixels. The softmax function calculates the exponential                the active pixel using fuzzy logic called the ‘‘fuzzy correlation
of the intermediate result and normalizes it so that it adds up to             maps” from the modification maps obtained in Step 5.
1. The resulting probability distribution shows the possibility of                 The fuzzy correlation maps are improved versions of the color
the input image’s pixels belonging to each output class. The class             correlogram, which calculates the color distribution of the altered
with the highest probability becomes the network’s final                       pixels. The fuzzy correlation maps compute a feature vector of each
prediction.                                                                    modification map.

fed to the CNN network. Based on the logic that as each picture                         image.
is unique, the correlation between the pixels creating the pic-                            It is worth noting that with the fuzzy correlation maps as input
ture is also unique, we select fuzzy correlation maps which                             to the proposed CNN, the output is the location of the pixels with

Table 1
Architecture of the proposed cnn.

  Layer                             Input size                       stride                   Kernel size                   Padding                  Output Class
  Convolution                       256  256  1                    4                        11  11                       0                        96
  Average pooling                   55  55  96                     2                        33                           0                        96
  Convolution                       252  252  30                   1                        55                           2                        256
  Average pooling                   55  55  96                     2                        33                           0                        256
  Convolution                       2250  250  90                  1                        33                           2                        384
  Convolution                       248  248  30                   1                        33                           2                        384
  Convolution                       123  123  30                   1                        33                           2                        256
  Convolution                       119  119  32                   1                        33                           2                        256
  Maximum pooling                   55  55  96                     2                        33                           0                        256
  Fully connected                                                                             11                                                    4096
  Fully connected                                                                             11                                                    2
  Softmax                                                                                                                                            2




confidential data, and the location algorithm we propose is sum-                  that our experiments to validate our strategy are conducted under
marized as follows:                                                               two scenarios: the payload location with a known steganographic
   Input: Fuzzy correlation maps of an inquiry image F, height                    payload and the payload location with an unknown steganographic
(h) and width (w) of the probable location of altered pixels.                     payload.
   Output: Location of altered pixels (AltP)                                         In this Section, we discuss the experimental setups and the con-
                                                                                  sidered performance evaluation metrics, obtained results and their
   1) Initialize F with SRM weights;                                              discussion, cross-steganographic-algorithm validation results, and
   2) # Iterate through the pixels in F and compute the loca-                     ablation study to identify the effectiveness of the components of
      tions with a probability of holding the secret data (prob-                  our strategy and compare our results to the state-of-the-art meth-
      able AltP).                                                                 ods’ results.
   3) FOR i = 1, number-rows DO
   4) FOR j = 1, number-column DO
   5) Compute the alteration costs Aðx; yÞ using (Eq (1));
   6) Let the probability as pa ðx; yÞ ¼ f ðAðx; yÞ; aÞ departing
      from (Eq(2));                                                               Table 2
   7) Store pa ðx; yÞ in M;                                                       Experimental environment setting.
   8) Initialize the AltP to zero;
                                                                                    Dataset                                      BOSSBase1.01
   9) #Consider the location with a probability of holding the
                                                                                    Image Color, format, and size                Grayscale, uncompressed, and
      secret data with coordinates of size h  w.
                                                                                                                                 512  512
  10) FOR i = 1, number-rows-w DO                                                   Dataset cardinality                          10000 images
  11) FOR j = 1, number-column-h DO                                                 Payload sizes in bits per pixel (bpp)        0:05, 0:1, 0:2, 0:3, 0:4, and 0:5
  12) Compute the sum of the probability with the upper side                        Steganographic algorithms                    HILL, HUGO BD, S-UNIWARD, and
      of the matrix of size w  h with the left corner of ði; jÞ;                                                                WOW
                                                                                    Data embedding and modification maps         Syndrome Trellis Codes (STCs)
  13) Extract the relevant information by performing statisti-
                                                                                       detection framework
      cal analysis on both the sums and their corresponding                         Fuzzy inference system (FIS)                 Type=’Mamdani’
      coordinates i; j;                                                                                                          Version = 2.0
  14) Identify the maximum value of the sums as Maxs ;                                                                           NumInputs = 4
                                                                                                                                 NumOutputs = 1
  15) Identify the pixels making Maxs based on i and j as AltP;
                                                                                                                                 NumRules = 81
  16) Save AltP in PGM format;                                                                                                   AndMethod=’min’
                                                                                                                                 OrMethod=’max’
                                                                                                                                 ImpMethod=’min’
4. Experimental results
                                                                                                                                 AggMethod=’max’
                                                                                                                                 DefuzzMethod=’centroid’
   This article’s primary contribution is locating the pixels altered               Payload locating scheme                      Our Proposed method
by adaptive steganography by adding secret bits. We use fuzzy cor-                                                               (Combining fuzzy and CNN)
relation maps obtained from modification maps of stego images to                    Considered locating schemes for              [20,27,31]

classify the inquiry image’s pixels through a CNN. It is worth noting

4.1. Experimental setup and evaluation metrics                                     (iv) The Acc is calculated using (8) considering the confusion

experimental environment setups, including the dataset we uti-                                TP þ TN
                                                                                 Acc ¼                      100%                                         ð8Þ
lized, the steganographic algorithms to embed the secret bits of                         TP þ TN þ FP þ FN
data, fuzzy inference system parameters, and the prior algorithms
used as benchmarks to the proposed method for payload locating.
                                                                                 5. Results and discussion
    The BOSSBase1.01 dataset [4], containing 10,000 grayscale
images with an uncompressed size of 512  512pixels, has been
                                                                                     The experimental results are arranged based on two scenarios:
utilized in this study. We use payload sizes ranging from 0:05 to
                                                                                 1) localizing the hidden bits when the payload size is known, and
0:5bpp, and the steganographic algorithms we employ for secret
                                                                                 2) localization of the hidden bits when the payload size is
data embedding are HILL, HUGO BD, S-UNIWARD, and WOW. We
                                                                                 unknown.
utilize the Syndrome Trellis Codes (STCs) framework for data
embedding and modification map computation. Moreover, we
employ a fuzzy inference system (FIS) of type ’Mamdani,’ version                 5.1. Payload location with known payload
2.0. In our FIS, we use four input membership functions (MFs)
and one output MF, with 81 rules connected based on the input                        In this Subsection, we first evaluate our method’s performance
MFs values with ’min’ as the and-method, ’max’ as the or-                        in locating a steganographic payload when the payload size is
method, ’min’ as the implication method, ’max’ as the aggregation                known. We use 10,000 images from the BOSSBase 1.01 as a bench-
method, and ’centroid’ as the defuzzification method. Our strategy               mark dataset described in Section 4.1 to generate the stego images
to locate the steganographic payload applies a combination of                    using the four state-of-the-art adaptive steganographic methods:
fuzzy and CNN, which is compared and benchmarked to other ste-                   HILL, HUGO BD, S-UNIWARD, and WOW. With different cost matri-
ganalysis schemes to locate the payload as proposed by Hu et al.                 ces, we generate six random bitstreams with varying stegano-
[20], Liu et al. [27], and Qiao et al. [31].                                     graphic payloads, notably, 0:05bpp, 0:10bpp, 0:20bpp, 0:30bpp,
    To thoroughly evaluate the performance of our payload location               0:4bpp; and 0:5bpp. To conceal the secret bitstreams, and we use
scheme, four metrics are considered, namely the Precision (P ðiÞ ),              the syndrome trellis codes referring to [14]. After embedding the
Recall (RðiÞ ), F1-score (F ðiÞ ), and the classification accuracy (Acc:).       random bitstreams, we generate the modification maps from
Our evaluation metrics are computed as of the following mathe-                   which we depart to generate the fuzzy correlation maps used for
matical expressions and based on the interpretation of our results               binary classification. To evaluate our method’s performance, our
departing from the confusion matrix in Table 3. TP represents the                results are computed based on three metrics: recall, precision,
number of altered pixels that were correctly classified as modified              and F1-score.
pixels, FP stands for the number of innocent pixels that were incor-                 Fig. 8 illustrates the achieved results in recall rate, which
rectly classified as modified pixels, TN represents the number of                reflects the ability of our strategy to locate the steganographic pay-
innocent pixels that were correctly classified as innocent pixels,               load when it is known to locate the existence of secret bits of data
and FN represents the number of altered pixels that were incor-                  correctly. As given in (6), it is defined as the ratio of the true pos-
rectly classified as innocent pixels.                                            itive rate to the sum of true positive and false negative rates. More-
    (i) The P ðiÞ is expressed as the ratio of accurately located pixels         over, this metric demonstrates the proportion of actual
with confidential data to the total number of pixels in the inquiry              steganographic payload accurately located through our strategy.
image, which include positive and negative predictions. It is calcu-             module. It is worth noting that at the start, the recall rate for the
lated as the division of the number of true positive samples TP, rep-            four algorithms increases in the same range and improves based
resenting the pixels that hold the secret bits correctly located by              on the payload capacity increase. Based on the same Fig. 8, it is
the sum of TP and the false positive samples FP.                                 identified that the recall rate in HILL and WOW is higher than in
                                                                                 other algorithms and that specifically, the recall rate with WOW
           TP
PðiÞ ¼                                                                 ð5Þ       reaches approximately 100% with a payload of 0:5bpp, which indi-
         TP þ FP                                                                 cates that almost all modified pixels can be located.
   (ii) The RðiÞ is calculated in (6) as the proportion of correctly                 Based on the trade-off between the recall rate and the precision,
located pixels TP out of the total number of samples considered                  the basic considered metrics to evaluate the performance of our
as TP and the samples of innocent pixels that are classified as pixels           method, we illustrate in Fig. 9 the F1-score as a balancing metric
holding the secret data FN.                                                      to avoid any imbalance due to miss interpretation. The F1-score,
                                                                                 which results from the harmonic mean of the recall and the preci-
            TP
RðiÞ ¼                                                                 ð6Þ       sion rates, is an evaluation metric that combines both the recall
         TP þ FN                                                                 and the precision rates to demonstrate the overall performance

is calculated using (7).                                                         method with HILL, HUGO BD, S-UNIWARD, and WOW under the

rate (FPR), which means that the rate of the payload correctly

all summative performance of our proposed method to locate the

steganographic payload, yielded with our strategy is 0.842. This

achieved with our strategy to locate the secret bits hidden by
